# Reliability Checklist: LSP Client Integration

**Purpose**: Validate SPEC-008 reliability requirements for per-language degradation, status visibility, bounded watch verification, restart/backoff behavior, and non-LSP indexing preservation.
**Created**: 2026-07-05
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are per-language degradation requirements defined for missing, unavailable-through-config, crashed, timed-out, malformed response, and shutdown-failure cases? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-038, Plan §Runtime Restart and Retry Policy]
- [ ] CHK002 Is failure isolation specified so one degraded language cannot invalidate other covered languages or the structural index? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Spec §SC-003, Plan §Runtime Restart and Retry Policy]
- [ ] CHK003 Are strict SPEC-008 validation failures for missing required real servers clearly separated from normal runtime degradation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010-FR-011, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Prerequisite and Version Evidence Policy]
- [ ] CHK004 Are non-LSP indexing preservation requirements defined for graph behavior, provenance semantics, and status/reporting surfaces? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001-FR-002, Spec §FR-016-FR-017, Spec §SC-001]

## Status Reporting

- [ ] CHK005 Are status requirements defined for detected, unavailable, verified, corrected, skipped-by-reason, and degraded language coverage in both human and JSON output? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-031]
- [x] CHK006 Are unavailable, skipped, and degraded status entries required to include enough reason context for users to distinguish missing commands, crashes, timeouts, malformed responses, oversized watch batches, and validation-only prerequisite failures? [Completeness, Spec §FR-031a, Plan §Prerequisite and Version Evidence Policy]
- [ ] CHK007 Are observed server version and selected-command requirements specified without making exact versions part of normal runtime success? [Clarity, Spec §Server Prerequisite Matrix, Plan §Prerequisite and Version Evidence Policy]

## Incremental Watch Reliability

- [x] CHK008 Are incremental watch requirements bounded with measurable limits for changed-file batches and per-language verification work? [Measurability, Spec §FR-035a, Plan §Activation, Config, and Watch Contract]
- [x] CHK009 Are restart/backoff requirements specified enough to prevent repeated crash/retry loops across watch debounce cycles? [Reliability, Spec §FR-038a, Plan §Runtime Restart and Retry Policy]
- [ ] CHK010 Are skip reasons specified when LSP watch verification cannot run because the changed-file set is unavailable, oversized, or language coverage is not applicable? [Coverage, Spec §FR-035, Plan §Activation, Config, and Watch Contract]

## Runtime Failure Isolation

- [ ] CHK011 Are requirements clear that malformed LSP data cannot produce speculative corrections or fail unrelated language coverage? [Clarity, Spec §FR-019, Spec §FR-038]
- [ ] CHK012 Are correction and suppression requirements reliable enough to avoid duplicate active edges while preserving audit metadata? [Consistency, Spec §FR-018-FR-018a, Spec §FR-029-FR-030]
- [ ] CHK013 Are shutdown failures treated as advisory runtime degradation rather than structural indexing failures? [Coverage, Spec §FR-038, Plan §Runtime Restart and Retry Policy]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK014 Are success criteria measurable for default-off behavior, missing/crashed server degradation, strict missing-prereq validation failures, parity failures, and correction ambiguity handling? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001-SC-007]
- [ ] CHK015 Does final validation evidence require both non-LSP preservation and LSP-enabled self-repo dogfood results? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-009, Plan §Validation Strategy]
- [ ] CHK016 Are reliability expectations traceable to the three vertical PR slices without leaving runtime degradation or watch behavior to a generic backlog? [Traceability, Spec §Reviewability Budget, Plan §Vertical PR Slices]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK017 Are local-server dependencies documented as user-managed prerequisites without auto-installation, auto-activation, or remote network behavior? [Dependency, Spec §FR-020-FR-024, Plan §Constraints]
- [ ] CHK018 Are command probing and fallback requirements consistent enough to avoid silently switching away from a user-configured but unavailable command? [Consistency, Spec §FR-036-FR-037, Plan §Prerequisite and Version Evidence Policy]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: failure isolation, per-language degradation, status reason quality, watch bounds, restart/backoff, validation-vs-runtime separation, and non-LSP preservation.
- Depth level: Standard reviewer checklist for Phase 4 reliability readiness.
- Explicit must-have items incorporated: per-language degradation for missing, timed-out, malformed, and crashed servers; status reporting for detected/unavailable/verified/corrected/skipped/degraded coverage; incremental watch verification bounds; restart/backoff behavior; preservation of non-LSP indexing; failure isolation so one broken server never invalidates the structural index.
- Remediation rerun: CHK006, CHK008, and CHK009 were re-evaluated after spec/plan updates on 2026-07-05; no reliability gaps remain.
